channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Sony Says it Won’t Sell Hardware ‘at Significant Losses’ as PS6 Costing $1000 Rumors Continue
item.content:encoded:
Sony has said that it doesn’t intend to sell its hardware at a significant loss as PS6 rumors continue to swirl that the console could cost $1000+.

The news comes following a rumor last week that the PlayStation 6 currently costs around $960 in materials.

PS6 Won’t be Sold at a Significant Loss

The news came during Sony’s latest Q&A session, where Sony was asked to provide an update on their current plan around hardware pricing and profitability.

In response, CEO, Hideaki Nishino, said, “First, we regard hardware as the base for providing the gaming experience, and by offering products such as the PlayStation Portal Remote Player (PS Portal), we aim to provide experiences tailored to users’ play styles beyond the living room, which has traditionally been considered the primary usage environment.”

He continued,

“As for pricing, it is not realistic for us to absorb all component cost increases, and we have already implemented some price increases outside Japan. At present, however, sales are proceeding as planned, and we do not believe this has led to a decline in customer demand.

“As a principle, we do not intend to sell hardware at significant losses. At the same time, we are carefully monitoring the market and continuing to evaluate our approach. We believe it is important for us to make every effort to ensure that customers fully understand the value we provide in relation to pricing.”

Traditionally, PlayStation has subsidized its console prices by a couple of hundred dollars in the hopes of flooding the market with consoles and making profits on software; however, it seems like the current RAM situation has made that unfeasible.

According to rumors, the PS6 is currently planning to launch in 2027 – a date that Insider Gaming could not verify.

That being said, hardware prices are expected to continue to rise, with some analysts predicting that the prices are going to double again in the coming months. So, at the moment, $1000 for the PS6 could be somewhat optimistic and could be a lot higher.

What do you think of the next-generation consoles and the PS6 potentially costing over $1000?Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming content, read about how there are ‘no plans’ to release a GTA 6 disc, and be sure to sign up for our Insider Gaming newsletter

Tom Henderson is Insider Gaming's Co-Founder and Editor-In-Chief. When he's not running one of the industry's leading independent video game websites, he's probably playing an FPS like Call of Duty,…

More from Tom Henderson

MOBILE APP

item.title: EWC 2026 Valorant Tournament Provides Ultimate Group of Death
item.content:encoded:
The groups for the Esports World Cup 2026 Valorant tournament have been unveiled, creating a brutal group of death for four of the 16 teams at the event.

The EWC Valorant tournament begins on July 2 as the groups kick off in Paris, before the Esports World Cup formally kicks off on July 6.

The EWC 2026 Valorant tournament sees Group of Death

The Esports World Cup Valorant groups have been revealed; they are:

*AG.AL International is the umbrella name for All Gamers/Anyone’s Legend used at the Esports World Cup.

With Valorant Masters London winners Leviatán absent, Paper Rex will be feeling good about their changes as the Pacific powerhouse eyes their first EWC title. However, their path could not be much harder, with Group B proving to be the ‘Group of Death’ for EWC 2026’s Valorant tournament.

EMEA’s best-performing team at Masters London, Vitality, are grouped with PRX, as are Valorant Champions 2025 winners NRG, making it a tall order for the final team, Karmine Corp, to survive the group.

Unlike the inaugural EWC Valorant tournament in 2025, the regions are not equally represented. EMEA benefits from an extra spot, awarded via the invite of last year’s winners, Team Heretics.

In EWC 2025, each group had one team from the Americas, China, EMEA, and the Pacific region, whereas EWC 2026 has one exception to that rule.

The extra EMEA slot means that China is underrepresented at the tournament with only three teams, meaning two EMEA teams in one group was inevitable, with Karmine Corp and Vitality drawn in the same group.

  • Fancy yourself an esports expert? Start betting with a $20 deposit at Polymarket and receive a $50 bonus!

The Esports World Cup Valorant tournament format

Two teams from each group will progress in a GSL format to the playoffs. All matches in the Group stage will be Best of 3 contests. The playoffs will see an eight-team single-elimination bracket with all matches Best of 3 except for the Best of 5 Grand Final.

The winner will collect $600,000 of the $2,000,000 prize pool. The tournament MVP will also receive a $25,000 reward for the achievement.

Like all events at the Esports World Cup, the event will distribute EWC Club Points, which are awarded to organizations. The points will decide the organizations’ placements in the EWC Club Championship, which awards $7,000,000 to its winner. EWC Valorant awards 3,350 Club Points distributed from 1st-8th place.

EWC 2026 Valorant runs from July 2 to the July 12 Grand Final.

Who will make it through the Group of Death? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out Liquid’s new roster for VCT EMEA Stage 2, and be sure to subscribe to the Insider Gaming newsletter

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

item.title: XBOX ‘Pausing’ Third-Party Game Pass Deals, It’s Claimed
item.content:encoded:
It was recently claimed that XBOX is pausing, or at least slowing down, deals with third-party developers tied to the Game Pass subscription service. This latest revelation comes following a couple of weeks of bad press at XBOX, punctuated by layoffs, price increases, and difficult discussions around the future of the ecosystem.

Game Pass was one of the first things scrutinized by CEO Asha Sharma when she entered office earlier this year, and she immediately set about making the service more affordable. However, it seems that another major change might be on the horizon, based on recent discussions, and it might impact the kinds of games available on the subscription-based platform.

Is XBOX Dropping Third-Party Developers from Game Pass?

The Game Pass subscription service is great, and that’s coming from someone who has had an unbroken subscription to XBOX Live and then XBOX Game Pass Ultimate for more than 15 years. I’ve been a relatively loyal fan of the platform, and I like to think I’ve saved more than I’ve spent over the years, given how many day-one titles I’ve had through the service.

However, a major switch-up might be unfolding before our very eyes if one recent claim is to be believed.

During a recent episode of The Business of Video Games Podcast, Shams Jorjani, the CEO of Arrowhead, hosted Fernando Rizo of Caboodle Games. The pair started talking about XBOX Game Pass, and Rizo made a rather damning claim.

  • RELATED: XBOX Reports on GTA 6 Pre-Order Numbers

He stated that he recently attended a trade show, and while there, folks were chinwagging about Game Pass deals, and some volatile terms were thrown around, including ‘rug pull’, ‘kaiboshed’, and ‘on pause’.

The quote was:

I was at a trade show in Italy, had some nice lunches, some nice dinners with industry colleagues.

Word on the street was that loads of people who were in the frame for Game Pass deals.

You know, nothing was inked yet, but the deals were in advanced discussions.

Everybody got the rug pulled out from under them.

When Jorjani asked if new deals were off the table, Rizo suggested that they’re ‘on pause’, but even that was more an assumption or a touch of conjecture than anything else. Rizo admitted that statement was ‘his read’, and that it ‘seems like’ new Game Pass deals might be on hold.

We have, of course, reached out to XBOX for any further comment on this situation, to help clear things up a bit.

Do you think XBOX is slowing down Game Pass deals? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming content, read about how there are ‘no plans’ to release a GTA 6 disc, and be sure to sign up for our Insider Gaming newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: Who Qualified for The International 2026?
item.content:encoded:
The teams for The International 2026 (TI 15) are all set after the conclusion of the regional qualifiers for Dota 2’s biggest and most prestigious event of the calendar.

The International 2026 will run from August 13 to August 23, with the 16-team Swiss Stage leading to a 10-team elimination stage, culminating in the eight-team playoffs at the Oriental Sports Center in Shanghai.

All qualified teams for The International 2026

  • Aurora Gaming (Invited)
  • BoomBoys* (Invited)
  • Team Falcons (Invited)
  • Team Liquid (Invited)
  • Tundra Esports (Invited)
  • Xtreme Gaming (Invited)
  • Team Yandex (Invited)
  • Team Spirit (EU 1st/2nd)
  • Team Vision* (EU 1st/2nd)
  • Nigma Galaxy (EU 3rd/4th)
  • HULIGANI (EU 3rd/4th)
  • Team Resilience (China 1st)
  • Vici Gaming (China 2nd)
  • OG (SEA 1st)
  • GamerLegion (NA 1st)
  • LGD (SA 1st)

*BetBoom and Parivision are playing under the titles ‘BoomBoys’ and ‘Team Vision’ due to Valve restrictions on betting site team names for The International.

Nine teams made it through regional qualifiers to add to the seven invited rosters, with the EU qualifier being, as usual, the most brutal and bloody.

The EU qualifier saw MOUZ, NAVI, Virtus.pro, and Yellow Submarine amongst the teams to miss out. MOUZ miss out on their first TI since Melchior ‘Seleri’ Hillenkamp’s retirement, while NAVI are left to question whether removing Pos 4 player Baqyt ‘Zayac’ Emiljanov in favour of Tamir ‘daze’ Tokpanov was the correct call.

NAVI and Virtus.pro both fell to HULIGANI in the lower bracket, a significant coup for the Tier 2 L1GA TEAM core, competing under HULIGANI for TI 15.

Nigma Galaxy recovered from a 1-2 defeat to Spirit in the Upper Bracket to defeat CIS talent pipeline Yellow Submarine to qualify for TI 15 in a vital result for the roster and organization.

The Chinese qualifier saw Team Resilience unexpectedly top the qualifiers, with Vici Gaming narrowly overcoming Yakatou Brothers in a lower bracket rematch. The result will leave Yakatou Brothers reeling after they had dispatched Vici 2-0 in the Upper Bracket, only to fall 1-2 in the rematch.

Elsewhere, the LGD roster are still riding the high of being picked up by an organization, and a legendary one at that, easily making it out of the qualifiers to make their first TI under the LGD brand after Heroic’s withdrawal from the Dota 2 scene.

Let us know who you think will win TI 15 on the Insider Gaming Discord


For other news, see why s1mple is facing a one-year ban by the Ukrainian Esports Federation and sign up to the Insider Gaming newsletter

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: GTA 6 60 FPS Graphics Mode ‘Very Likely,’ It’s Claimed
item.content:encoded:
Quality and Performance modes are fairly standard for most AAA games, giving you the option of crisper graphics or smoother gameplay. Given how mammoth Grand Theft Auto 6 looks and is going to be, the prospect of 60 FPS feels unlikely, but it’s apparently not impossible.

The biggest video game ever hasn’t stayed out of the headlines recently, that’s for sure. GTA 6 pre-orders are finally live, and there’s been talk about the AI in the game and the Ultimate Edition’s $100 price tag.

We’re sure to experience the most unprecedented amount of detail and depth in a GTA game (possibly any game) ever with the sixth mainline game, and it will look so much better with a higher frame rate.

GTA 6 Reportedly Has 60 FPS Performance Mode on PS5 and Xbox Series X|S

According to the Rock and Borys podcast, which correctly broke the news last year about The Witcher 3 getting brand new DLC in 2026, GTA 6 will offer a 60 FPS mode.

The podcast is in Polish, so here’s what YouTube’s auto-dubbed audio said:

“A source close to Rockstar, but I emphasize that this is a single source, but a very reliable one, it’s very likely, and ultimately, according to the information I’ve received, there will be two graphical modes: 30 and 60 FPS on PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series [X].”

They say that the Xbox Series S is being locked at 30 FPS right now, but they’re fighting to get it to 60 FPS. The podcast also added: “From what I’ve learned, they’re not 100% sure yet whether 60 FPS will be available at launch.”

  • More Insider Gaming: Full GTA 6 Map Being Put Together on Interactive Site by Fans

Curiously, nothing’s mentioned about whether the Performance Mode is locked to the PS5 Pro, with long-time concerns that the base PS5 model won’t be able to handle 60 FPS, and might struggle to even hit a consistent 30 FPS.

Recent news suggested GTA 6 will have ‘Improved Performance’ and ‘More Stable FPS’ on PS5 Pro, which makes sense. It also fits Sony’s narrative that GTA 6 plays ‘Best on PS5.’

Rockstar should eventually be able to tell us more about the different versions and what FPS to expect.

Fast forward to 30:10 in the video to see the exchange and info drop in full.

Do you think the base PS5 will be able to handle 60 FPS? Let us know via the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming content, read about how there are ‘no plans’ to release a GTA 6 disc, and be sure to sign up for our Newsletter.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Modern Warfare 2 & 3 Ports Rumor Has Been Shut Down
item.content:encoded:
Stories spread quickly on social media, and Call of Duty fans suddenly became very excited to hear about potential Modern Warfare 2 & 3 Ports. With other CoD ports in the works, it would have been cool to get newer versions of iconic MW games, but it’s not happening, unfortunately. At least not yet.

Call of Duty: Black Ops and Black Ops 2 ports are coming to PlayStation in July.

We know everything about them, including their platforms, multiplayer component, file sizes, and whether they’re remasters. However, before they were even released, talk of MW2 and MW3 ports emerged.

Don’t Count on Modern Warfare 2 & 3 Ports

Many Twitter accounts have been recirculating news that Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 2 and 3 are getting ports, similar to the upcoming Black Ops ports.

However, this rumor was swiftly debunked on the GamingLeaksAndRumours subreddit page.

  • More Insider Gaming: PS6 Reportedly Already Costs $960 in Materials to Manufacture

Two links on ORBISPatches take you straight to pages about Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 2 and Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 3. If you check the page, though, it says the pages were ‘Last updated 1 month ago,’ at the time of writing.

But Potential-Walrus-412 and several other users pointed out that these are ‘Modern Warfare II (2022) and Modern Warfare III (2023),’ and this leak actually surfaced weeks ago, and was subsequently ‘Debunked weeks ago.’

PlayStation Game Size confirmed as much, too.

So, no new MW2 or MW3 ports are happening then.

We’ve seen MW2 return with a remastered campaign, and all of its multiplayer maps remade for Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 3. Still, we wait on MW3 Remastered news.

If you’re eager for more CoD, then check out the Modern Warfare 4 trailer.

Is this disappointing to hear? Are you looking forward to Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4? Let us know via the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming content, read about how there are ‘no plans’ to release a GTA 6 disc, and be sure to sign up for our Newsletter.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Marvel Tokon: Fighting Souls Full Roster—Every Team & Character
item.content:encoded:
There’s a lot that goes into fighting games, but arguably the most important aspect is the character roster. A good line-up can make or break a game before it even releases, and for a franchise as vast as Marvel, it’s definitely tough to whittle the hundreds of options down into a complete launch package.

Well, Marvel Tokon: Fighting Souls is the latest Marvel game to tackle this, and the full roster of characters has now been revealed. It’s certainly a healthy mix of the faces of the franchise partnered with some more niche picks, but most importantly, the characters seem to cater to a variety of playstyles.

With the game releasing in August, it’s time to start thinking about which characters you’re going to main in this 4v4 tag-fighter, so here’s every team and character in Marvel Tokon: Fighting Souls.

How Many Characters In Marvel Tokon: Fighting Souls?

The base roster for Marvel Tokon: Fighting Souls will include 20 playable characters, each split into five different teams.

Arc System Works has slowly been drip-feeding character reveals in the lead-up to the game’s release, but it has long been known that 20 characters would eventually fill the roster. They’re split into five teams, which are as follows:

  • Unbreakable X-Men
  • Amazing Guardians
  • Fighting Avengers
  • Knights of Doom
  • Samurai Outriders

That said, as you learn certain characters and form your own group of four, the game will create a unique team name to fit your line-up! It seems to work based on your ‘point’ character, with the first word based off of them, and the second determined by the rest of the group.

  • More Insider Gaming: Everything Included In GTA 6 Ultimate Edition & Price

Some examples seen so far include Hellbound Mages and Cybernetic Fliers, with the point characters being Magik and Danger, respectively. Though this has no impact on gameplay, it can create some exciting team dynamics, but that all depends on the full roster, which you can see for yourself down below.

Every Character In Marvel Tokon: Fighting Souls

With 20 characters to choose from, there’s a real variety of instantly recognisable Marvel legends and more niche picks to fill in the ranks.

Without further ado, here’s every playable character in Marvel Tokon: Fighting Souls:

  • Storm
  • Magik
  • Wolverine
  • Danger
  • Spider-Man
  • Ms. Marvel
  • Star-Lord
  • Peni Parker
  • Captain America
  • Iron Man
  • Black Panther
  • Hulk
  • Doctor Doom
  • Magneto
  • Green Goblin
  • Carnage
  • Ghost Rider
  • Blade
  • Deadpool
  • Loki

Will Marvel Tokon: Fighting Souls Have DLC?

It’s been confirmed that Marvel Tokon: Fighting Souls will have DLC, with the Year 1 Character and Stage Pass already advertised.

Included in the pass will be four more characters and a brand-new stage. Odds are that they’ll form an additional team, though, as things stand, there aren’t any leaks about which characters could be added, or whether there will be future character passes.

  • More Insider Gaming: Does GTA 6 Have GTA Online Or Multiplayer At Launch?

That said, there are countless Marvel characters that could find their way into the roster; just take a look at Marvel Rivals and how that line-up continues to increase month by month—Tokon will be spoilt for choice.

Who would you like to see added to the Marvel Tokon: Fighting Souls roster? Are you happy with the base line-up? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming content, read about the latest regarding GTA 6’s Collector’s Edition, and be sure to sign up for our Newsletter.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: “I am the GOAT Support” Says Keria After T1 Destroy KC at MSI
item.content:encoded:
T1’s Ryu ‘Keria’ Min-seok claimed that he had become the greatest Support in League of Legends history at MSI after T1’s 3-0 victory over Karmine Corp to progress to the MSI Play-Ins.

The three-time Worlds-winning Support gave the statement in his post-match interview after being named Player of the Match after the KC series, where Keria’s impact was again undeniable.

As part of the POM interview following the 3-0 victory, the T1 Support was asked, “Keria, this is your fifth MSI. Compared to when you first stepped onto the MSI stage in 2022, what do you think has changed the most?”

Keria replied, “Back then, I really wanted to be the GOAT Support, and right now I can say I am the GOAT support itself.” (translated)

Certainly, there is a growing consensus that Keria is taking the Support role to new heights, playing a wide range of champions to an elite level, displaying outstanding mechanics across his champion pool, most notably on his signature pick of Bard.

The T1 Support is widely regarded as the most mechanically gifted player in his role, displaying world-class micro, especially in teamfighting, where T1 does so much of their best work.

  • Fancy yourself an esports expert? Start betting with a $20 deposit at Polymarket and receive a $50 bonus!

While Keria continues to grow his legacy in the game, those with an appreciation for League of Legends history may have something to say about players like Cho ‘Mata’ Se-hyeon, Hong ‘MadLife’ Min-gi, or Tian ‘Meiko’ Ye.

Keria seemed to have no regard for his own Coach’s claim to that title, with T1 Coach Mata widely regarded as one of the greatest Supports of all time, and a significant innovator in the space, and the only Support to be awarded MVP at Worlds for his outstanding play for Samsung Galaxy White in 2014.

With Keria’s current form, it is not impossible to imagine Keria equalling that feat at Worlds 2026, but first, the Support will look to qualify for the Main Stage of MSI, where T1 are one win away.

They will face the winner of Team Liquid and Karmine Corp on Wednesday in the Play-In final. KC and TL will contest the Lower Final of the Play-In on Tuesday morning.

Let us know your thoughts on the GOAT Support debate on the Insider Gaming Discord


For other news, see the details of the upcoming League of Legends Classic announcement and sign up to the Insider Gaming newsletter

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson
